extra.message: User input from params['id'][0] is concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). This allows SQL injection attacks such as UNION-based data extraction or boolean-based blind injection. No parameterized query is used.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The password parameter from params['password'][0] is concatenated directly into a SQL query string. Although the username is sanitized with re.sub(), the password field is not, enabling SQL injection via the password field. An attacker can bypass authentication or extract data.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied input from params['msg'][0] is returned directly as HTML content without any escaping or sanitization. This allows reflected XSS; an attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the victim's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-supplied comment data from params['comment'][0] is stored in the database without sanitization (line 88) and then retrieved and rendered directly into HTML without escaping (lines 94-98). This allows stored/persistent XSS; malicious scripts stored by one user will execute in browsers of all subsequent visitors.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User-controlled input from params['domain'][0] is concatenated into a shell command string passed to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. This allows OS command injection; an attacker can append shell metacharacters (e.g., ';', '&&', '|') to execute arbitrary commands on the server.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: The HTTP request path (self.path[1:]) is passed directly to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True in the do_BDR() handler. Any HTTP request using the BDR method with a crafted path allows arbitrary OS command execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User-supplied input from params['object'][0] is base64-decoded and then passed directly to pickle.loads(). Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows remote code execution; an attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload that executes arbitrary Python code when deserialized.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: User-supplied path from params['path'][0] is passed to os.path.abspath() and then directly opened with open(). There is no restriction to a safe base directory; an attacker can supply '../../../etc/passwd' or any absolute path to read arbitrary files on the server. Additionally, if '://' is present in the path, urllib.request.urlopen() is called with the user-controlled URL, enabling SSRF.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: User-supplied input from params['path'][0] is passed directly to urllib.request.urlopen() when it contains '://'. This allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF); an attacker can make the server issue requests to internal network services, cloud metadata endpoints (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/), or other arbitrary URLs.

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: User-supplied path from params['path'][0] is embedded directly into a JavaScript window.location.replace() call without validation. An attacker can supply an external URL (e.g., 'https://evil.com') causing the application to redirect users to arbitrary external sites, enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-supplied path from params['path'][0] is embedded directly into a JavaScript window.location assignment in SessionFixation.run() without any validation. This enables open redirect to arbitrary external URLs.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: User-supplied session token from params['session'][0] is set directly as the SESSIONID cookie value without any validation or regeneration. An attacker can fixate a known session ID before authentication, then after the victim authenticates the attacker hijacks the session using the pre-set known token.

cwe: CWE-384: Session Fixation

extra.message: The ExecutionAfterRedirect (Admin) handler at /admin returns sensitive admin content (Manage Users, Update Database Settings links) to any caller regardless of authentication. The JavaScript redirect to /login is only emitted client-side if no cookie is set; any HTTP client ignoring JavaScript will receive the admin content directly.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The XSRequestForgery (Settings) handler at /settings performs a state-changing action (UPDATE users SET email) via a GET request with no CSRF token validation. Any page can silently trigger this update by including a cross-origin link or image tag with the appropriate URL parameters.

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: The Clickjacking (Danger) handler at /danger performs an irreversible account deletion (DELETE FROM users) via a GET request with no CSRF token. Combined with clickjacking (no X-Frame-Options header sent), an attacker can embed the page in an iframe and trick the user into clicking the delete button.

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: The response explicitly sets 'X-XSS-Protection: 0', disabling the browser's built-in XSS filter for all responses. This security header should be set to '1; mode=block' or omitted to rely on Content-Security-Policy.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: Content-Security-Policy is set to "default-src * 'unsafe-inline'", which allows loading resources from any origin and allows inline script execution. This completely defeats CSP as a defense against XSS.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: No X-Frame-Options or frame-ancestors CSP directive is set in responses. This allows the application to be embedded in iframes by any third-party site, enabling clickjacking attacks (as explicitly demonstrated by the /danger endpoint).

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: SSL context options are explicitly cleared to re-enable SSLv3 (OP_NO_SSLv3 cleared), TLS compression (OP_NO_COMPRESSION cleared), and cipher server preference disabled (OP_CIPHER_SERVER_PREFERENCE cleared). SSLv3 is vulnerable to POODLE; TLS compression is vulnerable to CRIME. These options should not be disabled.

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: Plaintext credentials for multiple users (admin, guest, loella, weitzel) are hardcoded in db/users.xml and loaded into the in-memory database at startup. Passwords include 'Y3RFgwZP$v6v#D', 'guest', 'babygirl', '123456'. These are stored and compared in plaintext with no hashing.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: A hardcoded backdoor credential check exists: username 'dsvpwa' with password 'dsvpwa' grants login access without any database lookup. This hardcoded credential bypasses all normal authentication.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: The StaticHandler.find() method opens files using the URL path directly as './{path}' without normalizing or validating the path against a base directory. A path like '/../etc/passwd' or '/%2e%2e/etc/passwd' could allow reading arbitrary files outside the static directory.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: Session cookies are set without the HttpOnly or Secure flags. The morsel['path'] is set but neither morsel['httponly'] nor morsel['secure'] are set. Missing HttpOnly allows JavaScript to read the cookie (enabling session theft via XSS), and missing Secure allows the cookie to be transmitted over plaintext HTTP.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag
CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute
